statement.p: In a forest, the number of deer can be modeled by the function f(t)={\frac{300t+160}{0.6t+2}}\text{,} where t stands for the number of years from now. Answer the question with technology.
After 20 years, there would be approximately deer living in the forest.

solution.p: Use technology to graph the function f(t)={\frac{300t+160}{0.6t+2}}\text{.}
Locate the point whose x-value is 20\text{,} which is (20,{440})\text{.} This point implies there will be approximately 440 deer living in the forest after 20 years.
